title: Week 06 - Quantity Relations and Double Enumeration
description: Practice nested loops, solution search, and combination counting.

questions.title: How Many Ways Are There to Buy Two Types of Candy

description.background: A store sells two types of candy: fruit candy and milk candy. Xiaoming plans to buy total candies in all. He may buy any number of fruit candies and any number of milk candies, as long as the two quantities add up exactly to total. Buying 0 of one type is also allowed.
description.task: Count how many different quantity combinations exist when buying total candies across the two candy types.
description.output: Return the number of ways, allowing one type to have quantity 0.
description.hint: Enumerate the quantity a of the first candy type and the quantity b of the second candy type, then check whether a + b equals total.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(total)
    local count = 0
    for a = 0, total do
        for b = 0, total do
            if a + b == total then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: How Many Dice Outcomes Have a Target Sum

description.background: In a game, two normal dice are rolled at the same time, and each die can show a value from 1 to 6. Because the first die and second die are ordered, rolling 1 and 6 is different from rolling 6 and 1.
description.task: Count how many ordered outcomes have a sum exactly equal to target.
description.hint: Use nested loops to enumerate the first die and second die values, then check whether their sum equals target.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(target)
    local count = 0
    for a = 1, 6 do
        for b = 1, 6 do
            if a + b == target then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: How Many Length and Width Pairs Match a Fixed Rectangle Perimeter

description.background: In craft class, students use small blocks to build a rectangular puzzle. The teacher gives the rectangle perimeter, and both length and width must be positive integers. Count how many length-and-width pairs can make a rectangle with that perimeter.
description.task: Enumerate possible length and width values, count the pairs satisfying 2 * (length + width) == perimeter, and return the count.
description.output: Return the number of pairs satisfying 2 * (length + width) == perimeter.
description.hint: Use nested loops to enumerate length and width separately, then check whether the calculated perimeter equals perimeter.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(perimeter)
    local count = 0
    for length = 1, perimeter do
        for width = 1, perimeter do
            if (length + width) * 2 == perimeter then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count Stationery Plans for Buying Pencils and Erasers with a Fixed Budget

description.background: Xiaoming brings budget to a stationery store. Each pencil costs pencilPrice, and each eraser costs eraserPrice. He wants to buy some pencils and erasers and spend the entire budget exactly. Buying 0 pencils or 0 erasers is allowed.
description.task: Count how many pencil-and-eraser quantity plans spend exactly budget.
description.input: Three numbers: budget, pencilPrice, and eraserPrice.
description.output: Return the number of plans that spend the budget exactly, allowing quantity 0.
description.hint: Enumerate pencils and erasers, calculate the total price, and check whether it equals budget.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(budget, pencilPrice, eraserPrice)
    local count = 0
    for pencils = 0, budget do
        for erasers = 0, budget do
            local cost = pencils * pencilPrice + erasers * eraserPrice
            if cost == budget then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count Game Shop Plans That Spend All Coins on Potions and Shields

description.background: A player enters a game shop with the number of coins represented by coins. A potion costs potionPrice coins, and a shield costs shieldPrice coins. The player wants to buy some potions and shields and spend all coins exactly.
description.task: Count how many buying plans for potions and shields spend exactly the available coins.
description.input: Three numbers: coins, potionPrice, and shieldPrice.
description.output: Return the number of plans that spend all coins exactly.
description.hint: Use two-variable enumeration: enumerate potions and shields separately, then check whether the total cost equals coins.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(coins, potionPrice, shieldPrice)
    local count = 0
    for potions = 0, coins do
        for shields = 0, coins do
            if potions * potionPrice + shields * shieldPrice == coins then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count Ways to Split a Fixed Signup Quota Between Two Classes

description.background: A school activity has total signup spots to distribute between class A and class B. To make sure both classes can participate, each class must receive at least 1 spot. Count how many ways the total spots can be distributed between the two classes.
description.task: Enumerate how many spots class A and class B each receive, count the plans where the two counts add up exactly to total and both classes receive at least 1 spot.
description.output: Return the number of distribution plans.
description.hint: Enumerate classA and classB spots, then check whether classA + classB equals total.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(total)
    local count = 0
    for classA = 1, total do
        for classB = 1, total do
            if classA + classB == total then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Chicken and Rabbit Problem: Find Animal Counts from Heads and Legs

description.background: A cage contains some chickens and rabbits. Each chicken has 1 head and 2 legs, and each rabbit has 1 head and 4 legs. Given the total number of heads and legs in the cage, determine how many chickens and rabbits there are.
description.task: Use the head and leg counts to find the numbers of chickens and rabbits, then return both counts. If no valid combination exists, return -1, -1.
description.output: Return two values: the number of chickens and the number of rabbits; return -1, -1 when there is no solution.
description.hint: Enumerate the number of chickens. The rabbit count is heads - chickens. Then check whether the leg count matches.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(heads, legs)
    for chickens = 0, heads do
        local rabbits = heads - chickens
        if chickens * 2 + rabbits * 4 == legs then
            return chickens, rabbits
        end
    end
    return -1, -1
end

questions.title: Buy Child and Adult Tickets with an Exact Budget

description.background: A family goes to an amusement park to buy tickets. Each child ticket costs childPrice, and each adult ticket costs adultPrice. They have a ticket budget of budget and want to buy some child tickets and adult tickets while spending the entire budget exactly.
description.task: Count how many child-ticket and adult-ticket purchase plans spend exactly budget.
description.input: Three numbers: budget, childPrice, and adultPrice.
description.hint: Enumerate the number of child tickets children and adult tickets adults, calculate the total ticket price, and check whether it equals budget.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(budget, childPrice, adultPrice)
    local count = 0
    for children = 0, budget do
        for adults = 0, budget do
            if children * childPrice + adults * adultPrice == budget then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Find the Shopping Plan with the Most Items Under a Budget

description.background: A store sells two products, A and B. Product A costs priceA, and product B costs priceB. A customer has budget and wants to buy as many items as possible without exceeding the budget. If multiple plans buy the same total number of items, choose the one with less money left over.
description.task: Enumerate the quantities of product A and product B, find the plan with the largest total quantity, and return the A count, B count, total count, and remaining money.
description.input: Three numbers: budget, priceA, and priceB.
description.output: Return four values: A count, B count, total count, and remaining money.
description.hint: Use nested loops to enumerate countA and countB. Only consider plans whose total cost does not exceed budget, and update the best answer when the total count is larger or the remaining money is smaller.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(budget, priceA, priceB)
    local bestA = 0
    local bestB = 0
    local bestTotal = 0
    local bestRemain = budget
    for countA = 0, budget do
        for countB = 0, budget do
            local cost = countA * priceA + countB * priceB
            if cost <= budget then
                local total = countA + countB
                local remain = budget - cost
                if total > bestTotal or (total == bestTotal and remain < bestRemain) then
                    bestA = countA
                    bestB = countB
                    bestTotal = total
                    bestRemain = remain
                end
            end
        end
    end
    return bestA, bestB, bestTotal, bestRemain
end
